intake.topic_summary: Specify 17 missing subroutines in /Users/ghost/core/hive/tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa to satisfy proof-obligations of raw 118-134 (Q1-Q8 + T1.3 meta-self-host + R1-R8 paradigm raws). Specification covers: (a) 17 strategy names → _strategies() membership extension 25→42; (b) 17 slug→strategy mappings → _slug_to_strategy() routing extension; (c) 17 fn _check_*() bodies with marker discipline; (d) 17 dispatcher cases in _dispatch_tier3(); (e) raw 126 meta-self-host as second cross-cutting check (after raw 117 5-check), composing raw 117/118 into stability-bound gate; (f) 32+ selftest assertions following the established membership + positive + negative pattern. Deferred to Task A.2 sub-agent execution (drill remote-only constraint binds selftest verification).

alpha_absorbed.applied_to_design: absorbed motif = 'noise module physical/mathematical/architectural limits — tier-1 noise physical limit quantified'. Reframe: the 17 raw 118-134 paradigms collectively articulate the *theoretical-limit* layer of the design-strategy stack — Q-family covers EPISTEMIC limits (recursion, decay, counterfactual, chaos, topology, exploration, fault-tolerance, scale); R-family covers PHYSICS-INFORMED limits (game theory, RG flow, dp budget, Pareto, Landauer, operad, free energy, quotient). The lint subroutines must enforce that EACH limit's specification is mechanism-level (not vendor-specific) and quantitative (numeric thresholds where applicable). The motif anchors the per-fn marker discipline: every subroutine demands ≥3 distinct measurable markers (matching raw 106 channels) + at least one quantitative parameter (epsilon, lyapunov, betti, k-step, etc.).

axis_a.rationale: WHAT — the 17 fn _check_*() bodies as design specification (NOT yet emitted as code). Each entry locks the subroutine contract: name, slug binding, source-line target, field markers, severity, gating discipline. axis_a is the IMPLEMENTATION SURFACE; axis_b is the VERIFICATION surface (selftest); axis_c is the SELF-HOST surface (raw 117 5-check applied across 118-134). β bias (mod 17 = 14) prioritized R6 raw 132 ordering tie-break inside R-family — recorded but not material to the locked spec.

subroutines_specified.checks: self-app-witness contains markers {step_N=, input_hash=, output_hash=, fixpoint_status=}; fixpoint_status value ∈ {converged, diverged, bounded}; step_N must be positive integer; if fixpoint_status=converged, input_hash MUST equal output_hash (byte-eq fixpoint witness)

evidence-decay-half-life contains markers {half_life=, recheck_cadence=, evidence_age=}; half_life must carry time unit (d/h/m); recheck_cadence ≤ half_life / 2 enforces Nyquist-style evidence sampling; evidence_age ≥ half_life triggers refresh-required hit

counterfactual-twin contains markers {intervention=, actual_outcome=, no_intervention_outcome=, divergence_metric=}; divergence_metric must be quantitative (numeric); when no_intervention_outcome == actual_outcome the strategy is degenerate (raw 110 ablation regression — emit warn 'no causal contrast')

dynamical-regime contains markers {lyapunov=, kolmogorov_sinai_entropy=, regime_classification=}; regime_classification value ∈ {ordered, edge_of_chaos, chaotic}; lyapunov must be numeric (sign determines regime: <0 ordered, ≈0 edge, >0 chaotic — emit warn on classification mismatch)

persistence-diagram contains markers {betti_0=, betti_1=, scale_range=}; betti_0 ≥ 1 (every non-empty space has ≥1 connected component); scale_range must declare min:max scale parameter pair; warn if only single scale point (defeats persistence purpose)

exploration-policy contains markers {policy=, eig_objective=, exploration_param=}; policy value ∈ {epsilon_greedy, ucb, thompson_sampling, bayes_opt, information_directed_sampling}; eig_objective must reference bits/nats/info-units; exploration_param must be numeric

consensus-spec contains markers {N=, f=, threshold=, protocol=}; N (total parties) and f (max faulty) both positive integers; require N >= 3*f + 1 (classical BFT bound); protocol value ∈ {pbft, hotstuff, tendermint, paxos_byzantine, dolev_strong}

time-scale-hierarchy contains markers {micro_scale=, meso_scale=, macro_scale=}; ≥2 of 3 must be present (multi-scale requirement); each scale must declare per-scale invariant (companion field per_scale_invariant_<level>); warn if only single scale present

self-host-stability contains markers {k_step=, byte_eq_fixpoint=, divergence_tolerance=}; k_step positive integer; byte_eq_fixpoint value ∈ {true, false}; if false, divergence_tolerance MUST be numeric (≤ 0.05 default); CROSS-CUTTING: applied as second cross-cut hook in _flush_entry for every meta-rule slug (slug starts with `design-meta-` OR `design-rule-` OR contains `-meta-`)
subroutines_specified.gating_discipline: field-presence-early-return + cross-cutting hook for meta-rule slugs

subroutines_specified.checks: equilibrium-spec contains markers {payoff_matrix=, rationality=, solution_concept=}; solution_concept value ∈ {nash_pure, nash_mixed, correlated, stackelberg, evolutionary_stable, cce}; rationality value ∈ {full, bounded, level_k, qre}; payoff_matrix must be non-empty

rg-flow-spec contains markers {flow_direction=, fixed_points=, relevant_operators=}; flow_direction value ∈ {ir, uv, both}; fixed_points must list ≥1 fixed point name; relevant_operators must list ≥1 operator (irrelevant_operators companion field optional)

dp-budget contains markers {epsilon=, delta=, composition=}; epsilon must be positive numeric (≤10 typical); delta numeric in [0,1] (commonly ≤1e-5); composition value ∈ {basic, advanced, rdp, gdp, zcdp}

pareto-frontier contains markers {objectives=, dominance_relation=, chosen_point=, rejected_dominated=}; objectives must list ≥2 named objectives (multi-objective requirement); dominance_relation value ∈ {weak, strict, lexicographic}; chosen_point must be non-empty; rejected_dominated[] should list ≥1 alternative

landauer-budget contains markers {bits_erased=, energy_J=, irreversibility_budget=}; bits_erased non-negative integer; energy_J positive numeric; lower bound: energy_J >= bits_erased * kT*ln(2) ≈ bits_erased * 2.87e-21 J at 300K — emit warn if claimed energy < theoretical floor (physically impossible budget)

operad-spec OR n_category-spec contains markers {arity_signature=, n_morphism_level=, composition_law=}; arity_signature must be sequence of integers (e.g., '2,3,1') OR `n_morphism_level` ≥ 2 for higher-categorical case; composition_law value ∈ {strict, weak, lax, oplax, infinity}

predictive-hierarchy contains markers {top_down_prediction=, bottom_up_error=, free_energy_objective=}; all 3 must be non-empty; free_energy_objective should reference variational/expected/active free-energy framing; warn if top_down_prediction == bottom_up_error (degenerate equality, no error signal)

quotient-canonical contains markers {equivalence_relation=, canonicalization_fn=, canonical_witness=}; equivalence_relation must be named (≅, ~, syntactic-eq, semantic-eq, observational-eq); canonicalization_fn must be referenced (deterministic mapping); canonical_witness should provide example pair (x, canon(x))

axis_a.limit_class: HARD-bound (1:1 with raw 118-134 enforce declarations; raw 135-136 are different families — auto-absorption + sentinel-trailer, NOT design-strategy paradigms — confirmed by reading raw 135 line 3991 and raw 136 line 4037 headers)

new_crosscut.trigger: slug matches /^design-meta-/ OR /^design-rule-/ OR contains '-meta-' substring
new_crosscut.rationale: raw 126 mandates self-host-stability for EVERY meta-rule (rule that governs other rules), composing raw 117 5-check + raw 118 recursive-self-application — second cross-cut hook in _flush_entry mirrors the existing raw 117 hook pattern
new_crosscut.double_emit_guard: if strategy_filter == 'meta-self-host' AND slug == 'design-meta-self-host' itself, route through _dispatch_tier3 only (skip cross-cut to avoid double-emit, mirroring raw 117 pattern)

axis_b.rationale: HOW the 17 implementations will be verified — 35+ planned selftest assertions extending the existing pattern. Each new subroutine gets (a) membership probe via _is_known_strategy, (b) ≥1 positive (PASS) case, (c) ≥1 negative (warn-emitting) case. Plus 3 representative _slug_to_strategy() routing assertions for new entries + 2 raw 126 cross-cutting tests (slug matches → cross-cut fires; slug doesn't match → cross-cut skips). axis_b is HOW (verification surface); axis_a is WHAT (implementation surface); axis_c is WHY (self-host fixpoint witness). Pairwise correlation < 0.7 because axes test orthogonal properties.

selftest_fixtures_planned.form: _is_known_strategy('<name>') == true for each of 17 new strategy names; raw 118-134 each tagged
PASS: 4-marker complete self-app-witness; WARN: missing fixpoint_status
PASS: half_life=30d + recheck_cadence=7d (≤ half/2); WARN: recheck_cadence > half_life/2
PASS: 4 markers complete with divergence > 0; WARN: actual == no_intervention (no causal contrast)
PASS: lyapunov=0.05 + regime=edge_of_chaos consistent; WARN: lyapunov=2.0 but regime=ordered (sign mismatch)
PASS: betti_0=3 + scale_range=0.1:10; WARN: scale_range single-point
PASS: policy=ucb + eig_objective=mutual_info_bits; WARN: policy=random_walk (not in allowlist)
PASS: N=4, f=1 (4 ≥ 3*1+1); WARN: N=3, f=1 (3 < 4 violates BFT bound)
PASS: micro+meso+macro all set; WARN: only macro set; WARN: missing per_scale_invariant_*
PASS: byte_eq_fixpoint=true + k_step=2; WARN: byte_eq_fixpoint=false without divergence_tolerance
slug=design-meta-foo → cross-cut fires; slug=os-lock → cross-cut skips
PASS: solution=nash_mixed + payoff non-empty; WARN: solution=guess (not in allowlist)
PASS: flow=ir + fixed_points=[gaussian]; WARN: flow=sideways (not in allowlist)
PASS: epsilon=1.0 + delta=1e-5 + composition=advanced; WARN: composition=trust_us (not allowlist)
PASS: ≥2 objectives + dominance=strict + chosen+rejected; WARN: only 1 objective
PASS: bits=1e9 + energy_J=3e-12 (above kT ln2 floor); WARN: energy_J below thermodynamic floor
PASS: arity=2,3 + composition=strict; WARN: composition=magical
PASS: 3 markers + free_energy ref; WARN: top_down == bottom_up (degenerate)
PASS: 3 markers all set; WARN: missing canonicalization_fn

design-recursive-self-application → recursive-self-application (raw 118); design-edge-of-chaos → edge-of-chaos (raw 121); design-meta-self-host → meta-self-host (raw 126); design-canonical-form → canonical-form (raw 134)

axis_b.limit_class: MEDIUM-bound (positive + negative per subroutine + 1-2 boundary cases for the more complex specs raw 124/125/131; exhaustive marker permutations deferred to fuzz-suite follow-up cycle)

axis_c.rationale: WHY — raw 117 5-check applied to ALL 17 newly-specified subroutine targets (raw 118-134 entries themselves). Following the raw 109-117 pattern from prior Task A witness, each raw 118-134 must self-pass (a) genus-name no forbidden suffix, (b) ≥2 frameworks, (c) ≥3 channels, (d) ≥1 counter-example, (e) ≥1 falsifier. axis_c is the META-axis (the lint can lint itself + the rules it enforces); axis_a is OBJECT-axis (lint code spec); axis_b is VERIFY-axis (selftest blueprint). Triple is orthogonal across abstraction level. Validation method this cycle: by-inspection enumeration of raw-entry text in hive/.raw lines 3249-3990 (genus suffix check + line-count for frameworks/channels/counter-example/falsifier).

axis_c.fixpoint_class: 1-step deterministic by-inspection — N-step ω-saturation deferred to remote drill execution of `hexa tool/design_strategy_lint.hexa /Users/ghost/core/hive/.raw --strategy 5check-mandate` once Task A.2 lands the 17 fns; expected output: 0 tier3[5check-mandate] hits in raw 118-134 ID range (mirrors raw 109-117 self-host PASS in prior witness)

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a (subroutine signature spec, fn-line-target, marker discipline) ⊥ axis_b (selftest fixture blueprint, positive/negative/boundary) ⊥ axis_c (raw 117 self-host fixpoint witness across 17 raws by inspection). Pairwise correlation estimate: a×b ≤ 0.4 (selftest references the fns but tests their behavior, not their signature/line); a×c ≤ 0.3 (self-host check operates on raw entry text, not fn spec); b×c ≤ 0.5 (selftest has 2 fixtures for raw 126 cross-cutting, but those are HOW assertions, not WHY witness — witness lives in axis_c data block). All pairs < 0.7. Verified by inspection: no axis subsumes another.

axes_surfaced.name: implementation-stage-design-vs-emit

options_considered.option: A — full code emission this cycle (unlock + edit ~540 lines + relock + dispatch remote selftest)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: drill-remote-only memory binds hexa execution off-Mac; remote dispatch latency + verification round-trip exceeds single-turn budget; raw 71 falsifier-retire-rule mandates pre-registration BEFORE artifact emission

options_considered.option: B — design-spec witness with deferred code emission to follow-up Task A.2 sub-agent
C — partial emission (8 of 17 fns now, 9 in follow-up)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: splits the proof-obligation across cycles, breaks raw 108 absorption-mandate's 'one ω-cycle finding lands as one absorption' invariant; selftest assertion for partial 32-strategy state would need temporary placeholders

options_considered.option: D — emit code without selftest verification this turn
options_considered.rejected_rationale: violates raw 12 silent-error-ban (unverified code = false-PASS risk); raw 73 verify-then-seal mandates verifier execution before lock

axes_surfaced.decision: Option B — design-spec witness this cycle, code emission via Task A.2 sub-agent dispatched to remote ubu1/ubu2/herz
axes_surfaced.rationale: Pre-registers all 17 falsifiers BEFORE code (raw 71 letter-and-spirit). Honors drill-remote-only memory. Allows Task A.2 to run with locked spec contract — every check criterion in axis_a is the seal that A.2's selftest must hit, removing implementation-time judgment ambiguity.

axes_surfaced.name: raw-126-cross-cutting-mechanism

options_considered.option: A — _check_meta_self_host fires ONLY when slug=design-meta-rule-self-host-stability (single-strategy dispatch, no cross-cut)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: raw 126 contract scope: 'every meta-rule (rule that governs other rules) MUST declare self-host-stability bound' — applying only to the contract-defining raw misses the entire point (the rule governs OTHER meta-rules)

options_considered.option: B — invoke _check_meta_self_host as second cross-cut hook in _flush_entry, gated on (status=new AND slug matches /^design-meta-/ OR /^design-rule-/ OR contains '-meta-')
C — fold raw 126 into raw 117 5-check as 6th sub-check
options_considered.rejected_rationale: raw 117 explicitly scopes to design-strategy raws (any new design-* slug); raw 126 scopes narrower to META-rules; merging would over-fire 5-check on non-meta design-* raws; separation preserves both raws' contracts

options_considered.option: D — make raw 126 a Tier 2 cluster-wide check (alongside strategy-source / cross-repo-trawl-witness)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: Tier 2 is for raw-meta witness fields (raw 47); meta-self-host is per-raw content not cluster anchor; Tier 3 dispatch + cross-cutting hook keeps the layering clean

axes_surfaced.decision: Option B — _check_meta_self_host is a normal Tier 3 fn (dispatched for slug=design-meta-rule-self-host-stability), AND _flush_entry invokes it as second cross-cut for every meta-rule slug with status=new (gated to avoid double-emit when filter==meta-self-host)
axes_surfaced.rationale: Mirrors raw 117 cross-cut pattern with narrower trigger set (meta-rules only). Pattern is composable: future cross-cuts (raw 135 auto-absorption, raw 136 sentinel-trailer) can layer in without re-architecting _flush_entry.

axes_surfaced.name: marker-discipline-quantitative-floor

options_considered.option: A — every subroutine demands ≥3 markers + ≥1 quantitative parameter (numeric threshold or measurement)
B — uniform ≥3 markers, no quantitative requirement
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses raw 53 deterministic-verifier discipline — pure-string markers can't drive numeric_threshold/regex_ratio verifiers; raw 111 EIG-bits, raw 121 lyapunov, raw 124 N/f, raw 129 epsilon, raw 131 energy_J, raw 134 idempotence all need numeric checks for meaningful enforcement

options_considered.option: C — varying marker counts per fn (3 to 6 depending on raw complexity)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: introduces per-fn-discretion that obscures the cross-fn invariant; hard to schema-validate uniformly

options_considered.option: D — marker discipline only on R-family (physics-informed, naturally quantitative); Q-family allowed string-only
options_considered.rejected_rationale: Q1 raw 118 step_N is integer; Q4 raw 121 lyapunov; Q5 raw 122 betti numbers; Q7 raw 124 N/f — Q-family has equally strong quantitative floor; bifurcation would be artificial

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — uniform ≥3 markers + ≥1 quantitative parameter, enforced at field-presence parse time
axes_surfaced.rationale: Uniform discipline. Aligns with raw 53 verifier-type allowlist (numeric_threshold, hash_eq, set_membership). Empirically: every raw 118-134 already declares quantitative parameters in scope/why fields, so the SUBROUTINE just preserves what the SPEC already mandates.
